notes: RESCUE OP 2026-07-12 -- cost basis: MEASURED real rate 1.3998/1.3969/1.4004 s/step across three independent 10,000-step segments of the currently-running sibling job 200 (derived from checkpoint-file mtimes on /data, not log-read timing -- exact), stable across both near-idle (07:41-19:20 UTC, box mostly solo on this job) and fully-loaded (19:20-22:26 UTC, all 8 GPUs busy) periods -- ruling out contention as the driver. This independently reproduces SCALE_TRANSFER_DESIGN.md S5.11's own prior wave-3 disclosure (harvest 2026-07-07): measured 1.416 s/step on the same 1.31B/batch-16 config, a 1.985x miss against the same stale 0.7135 s/step constant, which self-terminated the ORIGINAL rung-3 ladder cells at 155k/183,105 steps (~84.7%). That lesson was never propagated into generate_jobs.py's B1 block before job 200 was generated (2026-07-12 05:27 UTC) -- generate_jobs.py has been fixed at the source in the same commit that adds this job spec. Priced at 1.40 s/step (rounds up from the max observed segment) x 183,105 steps / 3600 = 71.21 GPU-h, +2% frozen-bias overhead (S13's own <=1.2% measured overhead, rounded up, same convention as job 200) = 72.6 GPU-h. --internal-timeout=340000s (94.4h) is a clean 30%+ margin above the 72.6h expected runtime (the CLAUDE.md-mandated floor for a re-priced timeout after a miss). NOT a resume of job 200: lm_pretrain_rd.py's --init-checkpoint flag loads model_state_dict ONLY (strict=True) -- optimizer state is never saved to any checkpoint and the training loop always runs `for step in range(1, args.steps+1)` from step 1 regardless, restarting the LR warmup/decay cycle and re-reading the seeded dataloader from its start (confirmed by reading lm_pretrain_rd.py lines 1909-1976, 3094-3105, 2003 directly, 2026-07-12) -- so no clean resume exists, matching S5.11's own prior disclosure ('No optimizer-state resume exists'). Job 200 is deliberately left running UNDISTURBED (do not touch, do not pkill) to harvest its own partial/disclosed ~step-110,000-114,000 (~60-62% of budget) checkpoint separately when it times out and lands in failed/ -- that partial checkpoint remains a legitimate, loadable, disclosed-reduced-token reading and should NOT be discarded. This cell uses a DISTINCT ckpt-dir/out path (suffixed _pricefix) so it cannot collide with job 200's files. Comparator: reuses the ALREADY-EXISTING Track-C 1.31B checkpoint (SCALE_TRANSFER_DESIGN.md S5.11, harvest run 2026-07-07) as the arm_off baseline -- no comparator cell needed, same as job 200's own note. FOLLOW-ON NOT QUEUED (same reasoning as job 200): once this cell's final checkpoint lands, run lm_attractor_probe_rd.py against it (~0.5 GPU-h) to close the attractor-robustness-at-1.31B reading.
